summary: The new runtime proof is test-only and exercises the existing CLI once runner with the real local executor and an injected task-pull client; it does not alter production command execution, API, DTO, auth, token, schema, scheduler, daemon, web UI, or multi-instance behavior.

The proof covers the S003 terminal-runtime gap at the single-agent surface: contract/claim, command-step execution in a temporary cwd, step ack progress, final ack progress, and finish payload commandPlan/log/result writeback.

Focused API tests still cover claimed payload, ack progress/cancellation, finish writeback, and finish sync surfaces; focused CLI tests cover once/executor/finish, timeout/cancellation, ack renewal, cwd guard, and result payload behavior.
review_gate: Command execution safety, cancellation, and finish-writeback review.
